audit_status.source_base: Greek Revelation text tradition with Babylon/Rome/empire symbolism; draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for woman/Babylon, beast, kings, Lamb victory, waters/peoples, empire self-destruction.

verses.restored: One of seven messengers with seven bowls came and spoke with me, saying: Come, I will show you judgment of the great prostitute seated upon many waters,
with whom kings of earth practiced sexual misuse, and those dwelling on earth were made drunk from wine of her misuse.
He carried me in Spirit into wilderness, and I saw a woman sitting on scarlet beast full of blasphemous names, having seven heads and ten horns.
The woman was clothed in purple and scarlet, gilded with gold, precious stone, and pearls, having golden cup in hand full of abominations and impurities of her misuse.
On her forehead a name written, mystery: Babylon the Great, mother of prostitutes and abominations of earth.
I saw the woman drunk from blood/life of holy ones and from blood/life of witnesses of Jesus. Seeing her, I marveled with great marvel.
The messenger said to me: Why marvel? I will tell you mystery of woman and beast carrying her, having seven heads and ten horns.

The beast you saw was and is not and is about to ascend from abyss and go into ruin; earth-dwellers whose names are not written on scroll of life from foundation of world will marvel seeing beast that was and is not and will be present.
Here is mind having wisdom: the seven heads are seven mountains where the woman sits, and they are seven kings.
Five fell, one is, another has not yet come; when he comes, he must remain a little.
The beast that was and is not is also eighth and is from the seven, and goes into ruin.
The ten horns you saw are ten kings who have not yet received reign, but receive authority as kings one hour with beast.
These have one mind and give their power and authority to beast.
These will war with Lamb, and Lamb will conquer them, because he is Lord of lords and King of kings, and those with him are called and chosen and trustworthy.
He says to me: The waters you saw, where prostitute sits, are peoples and crowds and nations and tongues.
The ten horns you saw, and the beast, these will hate the prostitute, make her desolate and naked, consume her flesh, and burn her with fire.
For the Divine gave into their hearts to do his purpose, and to do one purpose, and to give their reign to beast until words of Divine are completed.
The woman you saw is the great city having reign over kings of earth.

translation_notes: Babylon is empire-city/system imagery, strongly associated with Rome in first-century symbolic register; do not use as arbitrary modern label.
Sexual imagery symbolizes imperial seduction, idolatrous economy, and exploitative power; do not misogynistically weaponize woman imagery.
The beast and kings turning on Babylon reveals empire self-devouring.
safety_note: Do not use 'prostitute/Babylon' imagery to demean women or sex workers. The symbol critiques exploitative empire and luxury built on blood/life of witnesses.
